description_blurb:
Apollo 17 was the ninth crewed U.S. mission to the Moon, and the sixth to land Astronauts on the lunar surface. This document is an excerpt from the Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing on January 4, 1973, in which astronaut Harrison Schmitt reported seeing light flashes.
•	Page 24-4. [Lunar Module Pilot Harrison Schmitt]: “We had light flashes just about continuously during the whole flight when we were dark adapted. I had one which I thought was a flash on the lunar surface. That one period of time when we had the blindfolds on for the ALFMED [Apollo Light Flash Moving Emulsion Detector] experiment there were just no visible flashes, although that evening, that night, before I went to sleep, I noticed that I was seeing the light flashes again.”
title: NASA-UAP-D6, Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing, 1973
summary: Excerpt from the Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing on January 4, 1973, where astronaut Harrison Schmitt reported seeing continuous light flashes during the flight, including one he thought was on the lunar surface. He noted the flashes were not visible during the ALFMED experiment with blindfolds on, but reappeared later.
reported_behavior: just about continuously during the whole flight; flash on the lunar surface
bust_reasoning: The document provides strong evidence for a mundane explanation. Astronaut Harrison Schmitt's description of 'light flashes just about continuously during the whole flight when we were dark adapted' and their disappearance when blindfolded for the 'ALFMED experiment' (Apollo Light Flash Moving Emulsion Detector) directly points to cosmic ray visual phenomena. This is a well-documented physiological effect experienced by astronauts, and the ALFMED experiment was specifically designed to study it. The single 'flash on the lunar surface' could be a misinterpretation of one of these internal flashes or a very brief external event, but the primary and continuous observations are clearly explained by known physiological effects.
full_text:
# NASA-UAP-D6, Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing, 1973 - Page 1

264 17

[redacted] CONFIDENTIAL [redacted]

1
MSC-07631

NATIONAL AERONAUTICS AND SPACE ADMINISTRATION

# APOLLO 17
# TECHNICAL
# CREW DEBRIEFING
# (U)

JANUARY 4, 1973

PREPARED BY
TRAINING OFFICE
CREW TRAINING AND SIMULATION DIVISION

This document will automatically become declassified
90 days from the published date.

NOTICE: This document may be exempt from
public disclosure under the Freedom of Infor-
mation Act (5 U.S.C. 552). Requests for its
release to persons outside the U.S. Govern-
ment should be handled under the provisions
of NASA Policy Directive 1382.2.

MANNED SPACECRAFT CENTER
HOUSTON, TEXAS

| INDEXING DATA | | | | | | | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| DATE | OPR | # | T | PGM | SUBJECT | SIGNATOR | LOC |
| 01-04-73 | M | MSC-07631 | R | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] | [redacted] |

[redacted] CONFIDENTIAL [redacted]

***

DECLASSIFIED
E.O. 13526, Sec 3.3(a)
NASA Declassification Guide
Date of Guide: (M) 05 (Y) 2026
Reviewer: PS
Date 5/6/2026



# NASA-UAP-D6, Apollo 17 Technical Crew Debriefing, 1973 - Page 2

24-4

[redacted]

EVANS (CONT'D) after the brightness of the fireball decreased, I could look
back up through the rendezvous window and see what to me was
kind of like a tunnel with a bright spot in the middle of the
tunnel. Way down the tunnel, way back behind, I could see
the fireball.

CERNAN The only unusual sighting I can recall during landing or
recovery is when the CMP looked out the window and saw the
superstructure of an aircraft carrier and said, "Oh, we've
got a tin can with us."

EVANS Well, it was kind of foggy on the windows.

SCHMITT Transearth we had only a small crescent of an Earth and it was
not feasible to do any extensive weather observations. We
had light flashes just about continuously during the whole
flight when we were dark adapted. I had one which I thought
was a flash on the lunar surface. That one period of time
when we had the blindfolds on for the ALFMED experiment there
were just no visible flashes, although that evening, that
night, before I went to sleep I noticed that I was seeing the
light flashes again. So, it just seemed to be that one
interval either side of it where the light flash was not
visible to myself or to the other two crewmen.

[redacted]
cover_up_reasoning: The document was initially marked 'CONFIDENTIAL' and contains standard FOIA boilerplate language. There are minimal redactions, primarily of classification markings and indexing data on page 1, and a single line on page 2, which appear administrative rather than concealing substantive information. The document also states it would 'automatically become declassified 90 days from the published date,' indicating an intent for eventual public release. While a later declassification stamp (2026) is present, the overall level of redaction and the nature of the content do not suggest active suppression of the core narrative.
title_zh: 美国国家航空航天局-UAP-D6，阿波罗17号技术乘员汇报，1973年
summary_zh: 摘录自1973年1月4日阿波罗17号技术乘员汇报。宇航员哈里森·施密特报告称在飞行期间持续观察到光闪，其中一次他认为发生在月球表面。他指出，在佩戴眼罩进行ALFMED实验时，这些光闪不可见，但随后再次出现。
reported_behavior_zh: 在整个飞行期间几乎持续不断；月球表面出现闪光
bust_reasoning_zh: 该文件为世俗解释提供了有力证据。宇航员哈里森·施密特描述的“在整个飞行期间，当我们适应黑暗后，光闪几乎持续不断”，以及在佩戴眼罩进行“ALFMED实验”（阿波罗光闪移动乳剂探测器）时它们的消失，直接指向宇宙射线视觉现象。这是一种宇航员普遍经历的、有充分记录的生理效应，且ALFMED实验正是为此目的而设计。单一的“月球表面闪光”可能是对这些内部闪光之一的误读，或是一个非常短暂的外部事件，但主要的、持续的观测结果可由已知的生理效应明确解释。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 该文件最初被标记为“机密”，并包含标准的《信息自由法案》样板语言。涂改内容极少，主要为第1页的分类标记和索引数据，以及第2页的一行内容，这些似乎是行政性的，而非旨在隐瞒实质性信息。该文件还声明其将“自发布之日起90天后自动解密”，表明其最终公开的意图。尽管存在一个较晚的解密戳记（2026年），但总体涂改程度和内容性质并未表明对核心叙述的积极压制。
claims: Astronaut Harrison Schmitt reported seeing light flashes.
Light flashes were observed continuously during the flight when dark adapted.
One flash was perceived as being on the lunar surface.
Light flashes were not visible during the ALFMED experiment with blindfolds on.
claims_zh: 宇航员哈里森·施密特报告称观察到光闪。
在适应黑暗后，飞行期间持续观察到光闪。
其中一次闪光被认为是发生在月球表面。
在佩戴眼罩进行ALFMED实验时，光闪不可见。
bust_explanations: Cosmic ray visual phenomena (light flashes caused by high-energy cosmic rays interacting with the retina or brain), a known physiological effect experienced by astronauts in space. This is strongly supported by the crewman's statement that flashes occurred 'just about continuously during the whole flight when we were dark adapted' and disappeared when blindfolded for the 'ALFMED experiment,' which was designed to study these very phenomena.

Brief external light reflection or glint, possibly from orbital debris or the spacecraft itself, which could account for an isolated 'flash on the lunar surface' if not an internal physiological event.
Micrometeoroid impact on the spacecraft window or structure, creating a momentary flash of light, though less likely to be 'continuous'.
bust_explanations_zh: 宇宙射线视觉现象（由高能宇宙射线与视网膜或大脑相互作用引起的光闪），这是宇航员在太空中经历的一种已知生理效应。乘员关于光闪“在整个飞行期间，当我们适应黑暗后，几乎持续不断”出现，并在佩戴眼罩进行旨在研究这些现象的“ALFMED实验”时消失的陈述，有力地支持了这一解释。
短暂的外部光反射或闪光，可能来自轨道碎片或航天器本身，如果不是内部生理事件，这可以解释孤立的“月球表面闪光”。
微流星体撞击航天器窗户或结构，产生短暂的光闪，尽管不太可能是“持续的”。
cover_up_indicators: Redaction of classification markings and indexing data
Later declassification stamp (2026) despite earlier automatic declassification notice
cover_up_indicators_zh: 标准的《信息自由法案》样板通知
涂改了分类标记和索引数据
尽管有较早的自动解密通知，但仍有较晚的解密戳记（2026年）
